zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Radcliffe於1282年出生在英國藍卡爾波利時，他的父親Richard de Radcliffe37歲，母親Margaret le Boteler33歲。他於大約1305年與Mayota在英國藍卡爾拉德克利夫結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。他於1318年被列為rector of bury。他去世於1324年在英國亨廷格多史爾波利享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Radcliffe于1282年出生在英国藍卡爾波利时，他的父亲Richard de Radcliffe37岁，母亲Margaret le Boteler33岁。他于大约1305年与Mayota在英国藍卡爾拉德克利夫结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。他于1318年被列为rector of bury。他去世于1324年在英国亨廷格多史爾波利享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Radcliffe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Ratliff
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from one or more of the places so named, such as Radcliffe (Lancashire), Radcliffe on Trent and Ratcliffe on Soar (both Nottinghamshire), Radclive (Buckinghamshire), Ratcliffe Culey and Ratcliffe on the Wreake (both Leicestershire), Ratclyffe in Clyst Hydon, Ratcliffes in Thorverton, and Ratcliffes in Broad Clyst (all Devon), Ratcliff in Stepney (Middlesex), and Rackley in Compton Bishop (Somerset). The placenames derive from Old English rēad ‘red’ + clif ‘cliff, bank, steep slope’ (see also Rutley ). A family of the name Radcliffe trace their descent from Sir Nicholas de Radclyffe. He is said to have been a knight who held the major of Radcliffe in Lancashire and served Roger de Poitou, Baron de Marsey in the 11th century.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Radcliffe was born in 1282, in Bury, Lancashire, England, his father, Richard de Radcliffe, was 37 and his mother, Margaret le Boteler, was 33. He married Mayota about 1305, in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. In 1318, his occupation is listed as rector of bury. He died in 1324, in Bury, Huntingdonshire, England, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Radcliffe est né en 1282, à Bury, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Radcliffe, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Margaret le Boteler, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Mayota environ 1305, à Radcliffe, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. En 1318, sa profession de son indiquée est rector of bury. Il est décédé en 1324, à Bury, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Radcliffe 1282 in Bury, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Radcliffe 37 und seine Mutter Margaret le Boteler 33. Er hat um 1305 in Radcliffe, Lancashire, England Mayota geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. 1318 ist sein Beruf als rector of bury aufgeführt. Er starb 1324 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Bury, Huntingdonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Radcliffe è nato il nel 1282 nel Bury, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Richard de Radcliffe, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Margaret le Boteler, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Mayota circa 1305, nel Radcliffe, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie. Nel 1318, la sua occupazione è indicata come rector of bury. Morì nel 1324, nel Bury, Huntingdonshire, England, all'età di 42 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Radcliffe(이)가 1282년 영국 랭카실르 버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Radcliffe(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret le Boteler(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1305년 영국 랭카실르 라드c리프에서 Mayota(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1318년, 그의 직업은 rector of bury이었다. 그는 1324년 영국 헌틴그도실르 버이에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Radcliffe nasceu em 1282, em Bury, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Radcliffe, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Margaret le Boteler, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Mayota aproximadamente 1305, em Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Em 1318, sua profissão aparece como rector of bury. Ele faleceu em 1324, em Bury, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Radcliffe родился в 1282 г. в местности бури, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Radcliffe было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret le Boteler, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Mayota около 1305 г. в местности радклифф, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. В 1318 году он работал по профессии rector of bury. Он умер в 1324 г. в местности бури, хантингдоншир, Англия в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Radcliffe nació en 1282, en Bury, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Radcliffe, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Margaret le Boteler, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Mayota aproximadamente 1305, en Radcliffe, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. En 1318, su ocupación registrada era rector of bury. Murió en 1324, en Bury, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años.
